name: @cf/openai/gpt-oss-120b
description: OpenAI's open-weight models designed for powerful reasoning, agentic tasks, and versatile developer use cases – gpt-oss-120b is for production, general purpose, high reasoning use-cases.

task.name: Text Generation
task.description: Family of generative text models, such as large language models (LLM), that can be adapted for a variety of natural language tasks.

prompt.description: The input text prompt for the model to generate a response.

lora.description: Name of the LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) model to fine-tune the base model.

raw.description: If true, a chat template is not applied and you must adhere to the specific model's expected formatting.

stream.description: If true, the response will be streamed back incrementally using SSE, Server Sent Events.

max_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate in the response.

temperature.description: Controls the randomness of the output; higher values produce more random results.

top_p.description: Adjusts the creativity of the AI's responses by controlling how many possible words it considers. Lower values make outputs more predictable; higher values allow for more varied and creative responses.

top_k.description: Limits the AI to choose from the top 'k' most probable words. Lower values make responses more focused; higher values introduce more variety and potential surprises.

seed.description: Random seed for reproducibility of the generation.

repetition_penalty.description: Penalty for repeated tokens; higher values discourage repetition.

frequency_penalty.description: Decreases the likelihood of the model repeating the same lines verbatim.

presence_penalty.description: Increases the likelihood of the model introducing new topics.

messages.description: An array of message objects representing the conversation history.

role.description: The role of the message sender (e.g., 'user', 'assistant', 'system', 'tool').

oneOf.description: The content of the message as a string.

tools.description: A list of tools available for the assistant to use.

name.description: The name of the tool. More descriptive the better.

description.description: A brief description of what the tool does.

parameters.description: Schema defining the parameters accepted by the tool.

type.description: The type of the parameters object (usually 'object').
The data type of the parameter.

description.description: A description of the expected parameter.
A brief description of what the function does.

parameters.description: Schema defining the parameters accepted by the function.

type.description: The type of the parameters object (usually 'object').
The data type of the parameter.

description.description: A description of the expected parameter.

raw.description: If true, a chat template is not applied and you must adhere to the specific model's expected formatting.

stream.description: If true, the response will be streamed back incrementally using SSE, Server Sent Events.

max_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate in the response.

temperature.description: Controls the randomness of the output; higher values produce more random results.

top_p.description: Adjusts the creativity of the AI's responses by controlling how many possible words it considers. Lower values make outputs more predictable; higher values allow for more varied and creative responses.

top_k.description: Limits the AI to choose from the top 'k' most probable words. Lower values make responses more focused; higher values introduce more variety and potential surprises.

seed.description: Random seed for reproducibility of the generation.

repetition_penalty.description: Penalty for repeated tokens; higher values discourage repetition.

frequency_penalty.description: Decreases the likelihood of the model repeating the same lines verbatim.

presence_penalty.description: Increases the likelihood of the model introducing new topics.

input.description: Responses API Input messages. Refer to OpenAI Responses API docs to learn more about supported content types

effort.description: Constrains effort on reasoning for reasoning models. Currently supported values are low, medium, and high. Reducing reasoning effort can result in faster responses and fewer tokens used on reasoning in a response.

summary.description: A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be useful for debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process. One of auto, concise, or detailed.

input.description: Responses API Input messages. Refer to OpenAI Responses API docs to learn more about supported content types

effort.description: Constrains effort on reasoning for reasoning models. Currently supported values are low, medium, and high. Reducing reasoning effort can result in faster responses and fewer tokens used on reasoning in a response.

summary.description: A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be useful for debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process. One of auto, concise, or detailed.

oneOf.title: Prompt_Output
oneOf.description: Output when using the Prompt input format

response.description: The generated text response from the model

usage.description: Usage statistics for the inference request

prompt_tokens.description: Total number of tokens in input

completion_tokens.description: Total number of tokens in output

total_tokens.description: Total number of input and output tokens

tool_calls.description: An array of tool calls requests made during the response generation

arguments.description: The arguments to be passed to the tool call request

name.description: The name of the tool to be called

oneOf.title: Messages_Output
oneOf.description: Output when using the Messages input format (Chat Completions API)

id.description: A unique identifier for the chat completion

object.description: The object type, which is always 'chat.completion'

created.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) of when the chat completion was created

model.description: The model used for the chat completion

choices.description: A list of chat completion choices

index.description: The index of the choice in the list of choices

message.description: A chat completion message generated by the model

role.description: The role of the message author (e.g., 'assistant')

content.description: The contents of the assistant message

tool_calls.description: The tool calls generated by the model

id.description: The ID of the tool call

function.description: The function that the model called

name.description: The name of the function to call

arguments.description: The arguments to call the function with, as a JSON string

finish_reason.description: The reason the model stopped generating tokens (e.g., 'stop', 'length', 'tool_calls')

usage.description: Usage statistics for the inference request

prompt_tokens.description: Total number of tokens in the prompt

completion_tokens.description: Total number of tokens in the generated completion

total_tokens.description: Total number of tokens used in the request (prompt + completion)

oneOf.title: Responses_Output
oneOf.description: Output when using the Responses input format (Responses API)

object.description: The object type, which is always 'response'

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) of when this Response was created

model.description: The model used to generate this Response

output.description: An array of output items generated by the model

id.description: The unique ID of the output item

role.description: The role of the output message (e.g., 'assistant')

text.description: The text content generated by the model

id.description: The unique ID of the function call

call_id.description: The call ID for this function call

name.description: The name of the function being called

arguments.description: The arguments passed to the function, as a JSON string

usage.description: Usage statistics for the inference request

input_tokens.description: Total number of input tokens used

output_tokens.description: Total number of output tokens generated

total_tokens.description: Total number of tokens used (input + output)

error.description: Error information if the request failed

oneOf.title: Responses_Async_Output
oneOf.description: Output when using the Responses_Async input format (Async Batch API)

object.description: The object type, which is always 'response'

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) of when this Response was created

model.description: The model used to generate this Response

output.description: An array of output items generated by the model

usage.description: Usage statistics for this inference request

input_tokens.description: Total number of input tokens used

output_tokens.description: Total number of output tokens generated

total_tokens.description: Total number of tokens used (input + output)

error.description: Error information if this specific request failed

oneOf.title: Stream_Output
oneOf.description: Server-Sent Events stream when streaming is enabled
